summary.headline: Three counterparties vetted on a Florida coastal property quota share. The cedent (Meridian Coastal) is financially stable but recently downgraded outlook; the MGA (Harbor Point) is clean; the TPA (Sentinel Claims) carries a RED flag for a documented pattern of claims-handling complaints and one open class action. All entities are fictional — this file is a builder test fixture.

resolved.identifiers: NAIC #99001 (illustrative); FL OIR licensed; S&P Capital IQ ID IQ0000000
resolved.note: Distinct from Meridian Coastal Insurance Agency LLC, the affiliated retail agency.

dimensions.label: Financial distress
dimensions.summary: Adequately capitalized with a stable rating, but the rating outlook was revised to negative in early 2026 on coastal catastrophe exposure and reserve strengthening.

findings.claim: Demotech affirmed the Financial Stability Rating of A (Exceptional) but revised the outlook to negative in February 2026, citing storm-season exposure.

sources.title: Florida carrier outlook cut to negative

dimensions.label: Litigation
dimensions.summary: Routine policyholder coverage disputes consistent with a Florida property book; nothing systemic identified.

findings.claim: Several individual hurricane-claim coverage suits in FL state court (2023-2025); volume appears in line with peers of similar size.

sources.title: Florida courts docket search — Meridian Coastal

dimensions.label: Customer reviews
dimensions.summary: Mixed policyholder sentiment with above-average claims-delay mentions during 2024 storm season; no regulator complaint spike.

findings.claim: Google rating 2.9 (180 reviews); recurring theme of slow first contact after a loss.

tables.title: U.S. Stat — P&C Financial Highlights
tables.commentary: Combined ratio crossed 100% in FY2025 (101.2%) and surplus dipped ~4% on storm-loss reserve strengthening; Demotech outlook turned negative. Coastal-cat exposure is the watch item.

tables.title: Schedule P — Incurred Loss Ratios (net, by accident year)
tables.commentary: Accident-year incurred loss ratios are drifting up (62%→71% at 12mo) with modest adverse development (+5-6% of initial), consistent with the storm-driven reserve strengthening.

tables.title: Schedule P — Paid Loss Ratios (net, payout pattern)
tables.commentary: Paid develops from ~31% at 12mo toward ~67% ultimate — a moderate property tail; the incurred-minus-paid gap is the case+IBNR reserve. Payout pace looks stable across recent years.

sp_financials.annual_statement: FY2025 statutory annual statement filed 2026-03-01; surplus down 4% YoY on reserve strengthening for 2024 storm losses; reinsurance program renewed with higher retention.
sp_financials.notes: Figures transcribed from Capital IQ Pro on the capture date.

resolved.identifiers: MGA; no S&P coverage; GA DOI producer license (illustrative)

dimensions.label: Partner relationships
dimensions.summary: Long-standing carrier relationships; no public disputes located.

findings.claim: Named program administrator for two other regional carriers since 2019; no termination or dispute reported in trade press.

sp_financials.not_available_reason: Private MGA; no S&P Capital IQ coverage. Financial standing not independently assessed beyond the open-web review above.

resolved.identifiers: Third-party administrator; TX TPA certificate (illustrative); no S&P coverage
resolved.note: TPA not named in the submission email; identified from the claims-handling agreement in the slip. Confirm with the cedent.

dimensions.label: Leadership & governance
dimensions.summary: CEO previously led a TPA that surrendered its license in another state; not independently disqualifying but worth noting.

findings.claim: CEO J. Doe was president of a TPA whose certificate was surrendered in 2017 following a market-conduct exam.

dimensions.label: Partner relationships
dimensions.summary: One carrier publicly moved claims handling away from Sentinel in 2024.

findings.claim: A regional carrier announced it was insourcing claims previously handled by Sentinel, citing service levels.

dimensions.label: Litigation
dimensions.summary: Open putative class action alleging systematic underpayment/delay of property claims; one prior matter settled.

findings.claim: Putative class action filed 2025 in TX federal court alleging a pattern of delayed and underpaid property claims (status: pending, not adjudicated).

sources.title: Smith v. Sentinel Claims Services — complaint

findings.claim: A 2021 bad-faith claims-handling suit was settled on confidential terms in 2022.

dimensions.label: Customer reviews
dimensions.summary: Strongly negative policyholder and adjuster sentiment with a consistent delay/denial theme; pattern is material for a TPA on a property book.

findings.claim: BBB rating F with 410 complaints, ~85% claims-related; Google 1.4 (520 reviews).

dimensions.label: Other relevant intel
dimensions.summary: Reported workforce reductions in the claims unit in 2025 may bear on capacity.

findings.claim: Local press reported a ~15% reduction in the claims operations headcount in late 2025.

sp_financials.not_available_reason: Private TPA; no S&P Capital IQ coverage.

limitations: All entities in this file are fictional and used only to exercise the report builders. TPA identity in a real run should be confirmed with the cedent, since it is typically not captured at submission intake. Absence of a finding reflects what was searchable on the report date, not proof of absence.
